title: Hello World
excerpt: The first post. Every project starts somewhere — this one starts here.
body:
Every project starts somewhere. This one starts with a blank terminal, a few lines of JavaScript, and a question: what's the simplest thing that could work?

The answer turned out to be about 60 lines of Node.js. No frameworks. No build tools. No dependencies. Just the standard library and a folder full of files.

There's something clarifying about stripping everything back to the metal. When you remove the abstractions, you can see the actual shape of the thing you're building. A web server is just a function that maps URLs to responses. A blog is just a list of text files served in order.

The rest is decoration.
